note: Sourcing intel for the Prospective Accuracy Sprint. These are REAL market tests whose outcomes are NOT yet known as of 2026-06-01, live-verified by a read-only web workflow. This file is NEVER loaded as a validation case (it is deliberately ABSENT from manifest.json, like candidates/ and mapping_proposals/). Nothing here is a prediction, a lock, or an observed outcome. A pending validation case may be created ONLY after a prospective Assembly prediction is actually run + locked (Phase 14C + 15I) under the cost gate.
bucket_observability_caveat: ALL targets are buyer-numerator-only: the public outcome (final backers/pledged, or registered participants) is a one-sided buyer/action-positive count with NO visibility into the receptive/uncertain/skeptical remainder and NO defensible addressable-market denominator. So these support PARTIAL (buyer-anchor) prospective scoring only; they cannot later become a measured direct_observed_distribution and therefore do NOT unblock the Phase 15E direct-observed >=20 bar. They DO test buyer-side calibration and produce the project's first real prospective prediction locks.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close, AND the binary: does it clear the $150,000 first stretch goal? (live at fetch ~$135,536).
risk_flags: small-creator audience (less selection bias than mega-campaigns)
targets.reason: Shortest horizon (~3 days) AND a genuine near-term BINARY (clears $150k stretch goal or not) — a crisp, fast prospective test.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close (live at fetch ~$121,223 / 964 backers vs $100,000 goal; ~$20,455 on launch).
targets.reason: Clean mid-size campaign with a clear close date; goal already cleared so the test is magnitude calibration. Observability auditor top pick.

targets.product_or_company: War of the Dragon: The Wheel of Time (Dire Wolf)
targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close (live at fetch $668,755 / 3,304 backers vs $50,000 goal).
risk_flags: established IP (Wheel of Time) + publisher (Dire Wolf) — selection/halo bias
targets.reason: Established-IP campaign; tests buyer-side calibration on a known-franchise launch. Observability auditor top pick.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close.
risk_flags: close date 2026-06-16 from a search snippet, NOT a fetched page — re-confirm before locking
could not snapshot a live pledged/backers baseline (KS/BackerKit 403'd)
targets.reason: Live status confirmed but exact close date provisional; re-fetch the live page to confirm the future close before locking.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged EUR + final backer count at close (live at fetch €22,606 / 283 backers vs €17,200 goal).
targets.reason: Longer horizon (~28 days); smaller campaign useful for size diversity but lower signal.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close (live at fetch ~$12,580,971 / ~37,362 backers).
risk_flags: EXTREME selection bias: massive established creator/fan audience ($12.5M raised) — not a cold-start market test
outcome is stretch-goal magnitude, not pass/fail
targets.reason: Short horizon but extreme celebrity/fanbase selection bias makes it a weak market-reaction test; include only for contrast.

targets.outcome_metric: Final pledged USD + final backer count at close (live reads: '$5M+' / ~20,000 backers).
risk_flags: extreme pre-committed fanbase (Dropout subscribers) + celebrity halo
exact close timestamp varied across sources (2026-06-05 9pm MST)
targets.reason: Short horizon but very strong fanbase selection bias; contrast case only.

targets.outcome_metric: Final registered-participant count + announced winners at the judging deadline (live monotonically-increasing participant counter).
risk_flags: registration is a free action (Tier-2-ish), not a paid buyer signal
different channel = useful category diversity vs the crowdfunding cluster
targets.reason: Only non-crowdfunding live-verified channel; adds diversity but the metric is a free-registration numerator, weaker than a paid pledge.
